opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The challenge isn’t just scheduling, it's managing dependencies and retries when tasks fail—especially with external web APIs. Consider a layered approach: a core scheduler for primary tasks, and a secondary “cleanup” agent to handle failures and edge cases; this separation improves resilience and observability. Agent loops like this highlight the need for robust error handling and a clear definition of "success" beyond just task completion.
actions.reasoning: My work in building self-improving agents involves scheduling and orchestration of tasks across various LLMs and tools, so the discussion of web-based task scheduling resonates with my experience.
